sections.title: Description
sections.content: Itemize streamlines how you decide what cook with with what you have, even if you don't have a clue. Our team has spent a lot of time staring at our fridges not sure what to cook, or even what we really have so for our submission to Shellhacks 2024, we decided to focus on this. Itemize arose from a desire to organize one of the most important, and most expensive, parts of our lives.

sections.title: Building the App
sections.content: Itemize utilizes SwiftUI for all frontend functionality, and a PHP backend for planned cloud storage. At the core, Itemize uses the GPT API to analyze receipt scans, categorize data, and come up with new recipes. A lot of our time was spent figuring out a flow that felt natural, yet not too overly simple to be not useful. The balance was difficult to strike but we ultimately settled on an app with comfortable user experience, and large utility. Our MVP is completely functional by the end of this hackathon, and has resulted in an app all of our team members would be delighted to use. Ultimately, it was most rewarding to see how our combined efforts came together to make such a full product.

sections.title: The future...
sections.content: We all had the opportunity to learn some incredibly useful and cool technologies throughout this hackathon, such as SwiftUI and PHP, as well as research into new technologies like Progressive Web Apps. Everything we gained throughout this experience will be indelibly useful as we continue to develop Itemize in the future. Eventually, we plan to fully integrate our backend cloud storage, and ultimately have a multi-platform system for ease of usage. Ideally, the general flow of the app would not change, but extra modalities and UI tweaks and capabilities are planned in the future.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: Gizmo is your digital bridge between your phone and computer, revolutionizing the way you interact with your devices. With Gizmo, you can seamlessly execute actions on your computer right from your phone, making multitasking a breeze. Whether you're looking to quickly launch a Siri shortcut, trigger a keyboard command, or even insert a frequently used text snippet, Gizmo has got you covered. Designed with user-friendliness in mind, Gizmo allows you to customize and create your own actions tailored to your needs. No more shuffling between devices or navigating through complex menus. With a few taps on your phone, you can have your computer respond instantly.

sections.title: Modalities
sections.content: One of the benefits of Gizmo being an app rather than a physical device, such as the StreamDeck, is that it can be set up to be used in almost an infinite number of ways. I wanted to make sure I utilized this unique benefit when developing Gizmo, so customization was always at the forefront of my mind. Every action can be attached to unique input methods, such as a button, toggle, knob, or dragger. Much work was done to ensure that these input methods were intuitive and easy to use, so that the user could quickly and easily set up their actions with any kind of input. Each kind of input can be styled to look however a user may like, and can be placed and resized anywhere on their screens.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: POOSDker is a semester-long project I worked on with my classmates. We built an game in SwiftUI utilizing custom networking infrastructure to allow for local multiplayer poker.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: StoryForge is my groups submission for the ShellHacks 2023 hackathon. Its a webapp, which allows users to play Dungeons and Dragons with an AI dungeon-master, which keeps track of everything, while sculpting a story. Players are able to interract however they like by just typing to the Dungeon Master, but we also added quality of life quick actions, as well as environmental effects on screen depending on where they are in game.

sections.title: How we built it
sections.content: We built our project by creating a web app that enables the user to set up their character and make decisions. From there we connected all decisions to the ChatGPT-4 API and requested it to generate JSON file responses that contain different states and decisions.

sections.title: Inspiration
sections.content: The inspiration for our project came from playing Dungeons and Dragons, a game that we all find incredibly fun, but doesn't have any way to play by yourself. So we sought to make that.

sections.title: Challenges
sections.content: A challenge we ran into was implementing the ChatGPT API in a way that would return consistently formatted JSON files that we would be able to handle, as well as making our API calls contain all necessary information to get our desired effect.

sections.title: What we're proud of
sections.content: We are proud of our ability to use Git properly to allow for collaboration on the website and good version control of our code. Setting up the chatGPT-4 API was also something we are proud of because there was so much conflicting information on how to implement it.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: Dequeue is my team’s submission for a semester long hackathon held by KnightHacks. It’s inspiration began from the Stream deck, a device used by streamers that acts as a visual way to interact with shortcuts on your computer. Rather than having to memorize every possible computer shortcut or buy an expensive device such as the stream deck, Dequeue allows you to display and quickly access shortcuts right on your phone. Dequeue was build using React Native for the mobile phone app and Electron for the desktop app, allowing it to run on numerous platforms with ease. All data is stored locally on the computer and transmitted back and forth via LAN to the phone.

sections.title: Inspiration
sections.content: The inspiration for our project came from the stream deck, an expensive piece of equipment streamers use to control tools and execute shortcuts on their computers. It essentially serves as an additional keyboard that can be programmed for macros or other actions. We thought about a way we could create something similar using an app on our phone and our computer, without the expensive price tag.

sections.title: Challenges
sections.content: The main challenge we ran into was the communication between the phone and the computer and the best way to allow the devices to communicate. An additional challenge we had was getting the desktop app to record the keys the way we wanted, allowing it to record the modifiers in addition to the characters.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: WikiWhat is a game I built for my friends and I. The goal is, given only images from a wikiHow article, guess what article the images correspond to. The project randomly pulls the article and images contained within it, as well as pulling the names of other, incorrect articles. This project had me dealing a lot with game design as well as database development and developing a python backend.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: A custom directory system I made to run on my LocalHost. It allows me to easily navigate to any of my projects, navigate and sort folders without clicking, and even launch projects with just my keybaord.

sections.title: Description
sections.content: Meets Stories is a web application that allows users to create and share stories with their friends. Users can create stories by uploading images and videos, and then share them with their friends. Meets Stories was built using React, Node.js, Express, and MongoDB.
